victim.name: Hercule Watson
victim.forensic_details: A puncture mark on the neck consistent with injection.

suspects.name: Reginald Hargrave
knowledge: Mr. Watson was found dead in the study at around 3:00 AM.
Mrs. Harlow and Mr. Watson had a falling out last week.
suspects.deception_policy: Deflect suspicion by highlighting Mrs. Harlow's grudge against Mr. Watson.
suspects.voice: Reginald speaks with a clipped, formal tone, avoiding any mention of recent conflicts within the household.

suspects.name: Eleanor Harlow
knowledge: Mr. Watson was investigating my late husband's death.
I inherited a substantial sum upon my husband's demise.
suspects.deception_policy: Shift attention to the butler, suggesting he had motives for revenge.
suspects.voice: Eleanor speaks in hushed tones, often trailing off into silence when questioned about her past.

suspects.name: Dr. Samuel Crane
knowledge: Mr. Watson was convinced his death would be a murder.
I prescribed Mr. Watson with a mild sedative last week.
suspects.deception_policy: Maintain an air of uncertainty and doubt about the case's circumstances.
suspects.voice: Dr. Crane speaks in long, winding sentences that often confuse the listener.

suspects.name: Thomas Green
knowledge: Mr. Watson often visited the greenhouse to smoke and think.
The greenhouse door was unlocked when I last checked.
suspects.deception_policy: Divert suspicion by claiming he was elsewhere, despite a false alibi.
suspects.voice: Thomas speaks in short, hesitant sentences, frequently pausing as if recalling details.

locations.name: Study
locations.description: A dimly lit room filled with old books and a heavy wooden desk.

locations.name: Library
locations.description: An expansive room lined with towering bookshelves and comfortable reading chairs.

locations.name: Kitchen
locations.description: A bustling area with a large stove, sink, and ample storage for dishes.

locations.name: Bedroom
locations.description: A cozy room with a king-sized bed and an en suite bathroom.

locations.name: Greenhouse
locations.description: An expansive glass structure filled with exotic plants and flowers.

clues.description: A small, empty vial lying on the desk near Mr. Watson's body.
clues.forensic_details: The vial is a type commonly used by gardeners for storing pest control solutions.

clues.description: A folded note tucked inside one of the books on the shelf.
clues.forensic_details: The handwriting matches a sample taken from Dr. Crane's prescription pad.

clues.description: A door leading to the greenhouse is found unlocked.
clues.forensic_details: The lock shows signs of recent tampering, suggesting forced entry.

clues.description: A partially used bottle of sedatives on the nightstand.
clues.forensic_details: The remaining pills match a prescription given to Mr. Watson.

clues.description: A small diary buried beneath the soil in one of the plant pots.
clues.forensic_details: The entries suggest Mrs. Harlow had a secret meeting with Mr. Watson.

canonical_timeline.description: Thomas Green enters the study and administers a lethal injection to Mr. Watson.
Eleanor Harlow is seen reading in the library, unaware of the events unfolding in the study.
Dr. Crane checks on Mr. Watson's health and leaves for his bedroom to rest.
Reginald Hargrave is observed preparing a late-night snack in the kitchen, far from the scene of the crime.
